formations_blocks: Iraq possession phase with a compact, physical defensive block
France sustained early pressure in advanced areas
France’s attacking setup with Olise as a No. 10
France combination play on the right between Dembélé and Koundé
adjustments: Olise appears to have started at No. 10
Mbappé repeatedly drifting/receiving to turn and attack space
France quickly establishing territory after Iraq’s early long touch
key_matchups: Dembélé and Koundé combination on the right
Zidane Iqbal carrying deep against Manu Koné
phases.summary: Iraq began with some possession and even showed confidence on the ball, but France were the more dangerous side immediately, getting into the area early and repeatedly finding Mbappé between the lines. The key pattern was France’s quick attacks and central creativity through Olise, while Iraq’s physical approach, including an early booking for al-Ammari on Mbappé, tried to disrupt the rhythm before Mbappé’s 14th-minute strike broke the deadlock.

formations_blocks: France attacking in waves / sustained attacking pressure
Iraq into their shell then responding with attacking spells
adjustments: 25' Iraq substitution: Aymen Hussein off with a groin strain, Ali al-Hamadi on
Iraq's approach became more proactive after the break/hydration pause, with more quick passing and attacks down the left via Doski
key_matchups: Ali al-Hamadi vs France's defensive marking (Kone mentioned in a shirt-pull)
phases.summary: France dominated the phase with repeated waves of attack, led by Mbappé, Olise and Barcola, forcing Iraq deeper while still allowing the visitors occasional forays forward. Iraq absorbed pressure, then showed more ambition after the break in play, with the substitute Ali al-Hamadi immediately involved in aerial and attacking situations, plus a corner and left-side crossing move through Doski.

key_matchups: Ahmed Basil vs Mbappé's shot for France's goal
phases.summary: The commentary provides very little tactical detail beyond France leading 1-0 at halftime through Mbappé's 14th-minute goal. The main football-specific note is that Ahmed Basil was beaten by a shot that arrived quicker than expected rather than from a difficult angle, while the rest of the phase is dominated by the storm delay and evacuation updates.

formations_blocks: France circulating possession against a compact Iraqi side
Iraq building out from the back in a structured passing shape
France threatening in transition after Iraq lose the ball
Iraq forced into a more passive shape after the second goal, struggling to get past halfway
adjustments: Iraq continued to play out from the back despite repeated errors
France used wide threats through Mbappé on the left, Barcola on the left, and Dembélé on the right
Iraq made two substitutions at 60' with Rebin Sulaka and Youssef Amyn replacing Zaid Tahseen and Zaid Ismael
France’s pressing/transition game punished Iraq’s back-line mistakes leading to two goals before and at 66'
key_matchups: Mbappé vs the Iraqi back line
Dembélé vs the Iraqi defenders on the right
phases.summary: This phase was defined by France controlling the ball while Iraq tried to keep building from the back, but their passing errors repeatedly invited French pressure and goals. Iraq’s early composure gave way to a more subdued spell after 54', with France exploiting width and transitions through Mbappé, Dembélé and Olise to put the game beyond reach.

formations_blocks: Iraq best spell but still effectively in a defending/containment phase
France pushing upfield and attacking in waves
adjustments: France continue to rely on Mbappé carrying from the left before Deschamps eventually sub him off at 90'
Barcola drives into the box as another direct dribbler/cutback outlet
Cherki is used in advanced one-on-one dribbling situations
key_matchups: Mbappé vs Iraq defender in the left-sided drive at 81'
Barcola vs Iraq defense on the right/inside channel for the cutback
Mbappé vs keeper on the offside shot at 87'
phases.summary: Iraq enjoyed their best spell of the match in this late phase, but it was still not enough to seriously trouble Maignan or change the outcome. France increasingly managed the game through direct individual quality, with Mbappé and Barcola repeatedly getting into dangerous areas before France finished comfortably and Deschamps removed Mbappé at 90'.
